message:
fix(core): skip implicit aggregate row injection when HAVING clause is present

The VDBE implicit-aggregate fallback that injects a single row with
default aggregate values (COUNT→0, TOTAL→0.0) when the WHERE clause
filters out all rows was incorrectly firing even when a HAVING clause
was present. A HAVING clause that filters out the aggregate row should
produce an empty result set, not a default-value row — the HAVING
clause's purpose is to conditionally exclude aggregate results.

- Add a guard that skips the implicit aggregate row injection when the
  SELECT has a HAVING clause, letting the empty result propagate correctly
- Add comment explaining the interaction between HAVING and the implicit
  aggregate optimization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
